name: Hitachi Class 800
funFact: An advanced bi-mode train that transitions seamlessly from pulling clean power from overhead electric wires to firing up under-floor diesel engines.

name: Amtrak Airo Cascades
funFact: The Cascades Airo set pairs Venture-style coaches with new Charger-family motive power for a six-car corridor trainset.

name: Empire Builder Superliner Set
funFact: A full Empire Builder consist typically relies on two ALC-42 locomotives hauling a long string of Superliners across some of the most remote intercity territory in the country.

name: Acela Express
funFact: Each original Acela trainset used electric power cars permanently coupled to a set of six trailer cars, making it Amtrak's first modern high-speed flagship.

name: Union Pacific 4000 'Big Boy'
funFact: Built in 1941, this is the largest and heaviest operational steam locomotive in history, constructed specifically to haul heavy freight over the Wasatch mountains.

name: CAF Oaris
funFact: Spain's modular multiple-unit prototype capable of custom internal formatting across high-density regional layouts.

name: Siemens Charger Venture
funFact: The Venture trainset uses semi-permanently coupled coaches, cutting dwell time and making corridor service feel more like a fast metro than classic rail.

name: Siemens Venture (Gold Runner)
funFact: This California configuration is a seven-car Venture trainset with a cab car, designed to run with separate diesel-electric locomotives.

name: Hyundai Rotem KTX-Eum
funFact: South Korea's modern electric multiple-unit option that accelerates smoothly compared with aging engine-car train setups.

name: EMD F7 Locomotive Set
funFact: The absolute classic face of mid-century American railroading, its streamlined 'bulldog' nose design pulled famous luxury streamliners nationwide.

name: Stadler FLIRT H2
funFact: Instead of a diesel engine, this train carries hydrogen tanks and fuel cells, producing only water vapor at the tailpipe.

name: Talgo Series 8
funFact: Amtrak Cascades uses these lightweight articulated trainsets, with Oregon owning two distinctive Series 8 sets.

name: Stadler SMILE (Euroduplex alternative)
funFact: The first high-speed single-deck train explicitly structured to safely traverse the long subterranean Gotthard Base Tunnel under Switzerland.

name: TGV POS (SNCF)
funFact: A highly modified version of this specific French trainset achieved a staggering world record speed of 357.2 mph over standard railway tracks.

name: JR Central L0 Series (Maglev)
funFact: This train levitates 4 inches above the track using superconducting magnets, holding the world land speed record for rail at 375 mph.

name: Siemens Velaro (ICE 3)
funFact: Germany's signature engineering masterpiece distributed all motor equipment entirely beneath the passenger cabins, creating window lounges at both ends.

name: Amtrak Airo Northeast Regional
funFact: This Airo variant uses a power car to pull electricity from overhead lines and feed the trainset's traction motors.

name: Shanghai Transrapid
funFact: The first commercial high-speed magnetic levitation line ever opened globally, linking Shanghai's airport directly into the urban subway network.

name: Alstom Avelia Liberty
funFact: Its articulated design shares wheelsets between cars, reducing drag and making very high-speed trips smoother through curves.

name: Siemens Viaggio Comfort
funFact: These train cars feature pressure-sealed cabins so that passengers' ears don't 'pop' when entering tunnels at high speeds.

name: British Rail Class 43 (HST)
funFact: Affectionately named the InterCity 125, it remains the fastest diesel-powered production train in rail history.

name: Chuo Shinkansen L0 Series (Production Standard)
funFact: The ultimate evolution of high-speed rail, designed to operate inside tunnels cutting directly through the Japanese Alps.

name: Alstom AGV
funFact: Combines articulated carriage architecture alongside highly advanced distributed traction design for superior energy conservation.

name: Talgo Avril
funFact: Utilizes a smart ultra-low-profile continuous floor layout configuration that allows 3x2 high-density seating on variable-gauge axle tracks.

name: CR400AF Fuxing Hao
funFact: One of the fastest conventional commercial high-speed trains in active operation, serving the highly active Beijing–Shanghai main trunk lines.

name: Alstom Euroduplex (TGV)
funFact: This is the world's only double-decker high-speed train, designed to move massive crowds across Europe at triple-digit speeds.

name: Series 0 Shinkansen
funFact: The grandfather of high-speed rail, this iconic 'bullet train' launched in Japan in 1964 just in time for the Tokyo Olympic Games.

name: Sounder BiLevel Set
funFact: A Sounder set combines an EMD F59PHI with seven Bombardier BiLevel coaches, giving it massive commuter seating compared with typical intercity trainsets.

name: JR East E5 Series
funFact: Features a radically elongated 49-foot aerodynamic nose design deliberately formed to minimize tunnel boom noise across mountainous Japanese routes.

name: Shinkansen E4 Series Max
funFact: This double-deck giant holds the historic record for the highest passenger capacity of any high-speed train ever manufactured.
